## [00:00 - 03:00]

What's it mean to you? The reason I love the word recalibrate is because there's the analogy of the satellite when the satellite gets shot into space and then it's going to get to its destination. It has to continuously recalibrate where it currently is and calibrate itself so that it gets back onto the track that it's meant to be on to get to the destination where it gets to. So like every, every moment, every second, there's a point two degrees to the left, point three degrees to the right. And I find that that's so amazing that a satellite readjusts itself every single minute. Whereas what about human beings? When's the last time you've recalibrated yourself to your current values that you have? And I realized this in 2016 when I was chasing my goals that I'd set when I was 18 years old and I didn't stop for the last eight years to recalibrate. So I was chasing stupid goals that I set when I was 18. You know, the stupid, oh, I have to make a million dollars. It's like, well, why do you have to make a million dollars? So I found myself being guided by goals that were created by a previous version of me. And I found that was so dangerous because I think that's one of the main reasons why people reach destinations they don't want to actually go to. You know, and that that to me is what recalibrate is. It's stopping and going then in 2021. What does he actually want? What does he actually care about? And where you're going? Is that still where you want to go? Yeah, there's that quote that analogy where I think maybe it might be marine related. If that GPS is off just by one degree, that's a good point.
